title: jasione humilis subsp. mariana
intro: Jasione humilis subsp. mariana, also known as the Mountain Bluebells, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the bellflower family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and calm irritated skin and wounds. The health benefits of Jasione humilis subsp. mariana include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and act as an antioxidant. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and topical creams, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and optimal usage.
